summary: Perampanel has no proven benefit in ALS due to the complete absence of clinical trials or studies assessing its effectiveness or safety in ALS patients. Although it inhibits AMPA receptor-mediated excitation—potentially related to ALS pathology—it does not have an approved indication nor supported outcomes in this condition; thus, it should not be recommended for use in ALS.

Answer choice: B
